behavior_in_task_description.reason: All paths, tool names (Prisma-Postgres-account-status etc.), core tools (migrate-status, migrate-dev, migrate-reset, Prisma-Studio), keyword 'MCP', README requirements, and relative import paths are explicitly stated in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to move/change but doesn't give exact sed commands or line numbers; agent must figure out how to perform the move and edits.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for file existence, import paths, tool registrations, and keyword presence — multiple valid implementations would pass; no gold-specific variable names tested.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Node scripts that parse imports, run prettier/eslint/check-engines-override subprocesses, verifying behavior and toolchain compliance.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each test checks a specific required change (file moved, PDP tools removed, keyword added, README created); a stub cannot satisfy them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five instruction requirements (move, import updates, PDP removal, keyword, README) are tested, plus core tools preserved.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass core tools, repo CI/CD checks) and named descriptively.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces code changes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to move (which is intrinsic to the task) but does not pinpoint specific lines to change.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Not an agentmd-edits task; package.json edit is minimal keyword addition.
